title: The Docket - Week 12
subject: Quarter 2 is done. Here's every legal AI trend that actually mattered.
preheader: The 5 stories that shaped legal AI this quarter, plus what's coming in Q3
bodyHtml:
The Docket

LegalTech AI Hub · Q2 Wrap-Up · June 17, 2026

Twelve weeks of The Docket. One quarter of legal AI news. Here’s the signal through the noise — the five developments that actually mattered, and what to watch in Q3.

Q2 2026: The 5 Stories That Mattered

1. The Trust Breach

A vendor training on client data without consent changed the conversation overnight. Every firm now asks about data handling before signing. This was the maturation moment the industry needed.

2. Malpractice Insurance Gets Real

AI exclusions in malpractice policies moved from theoretical to actual. Firms that don’t document their AI verification workflows now face coverage gaps. This is the forcing function for responsible adoption.

3. Platform Consolidation Accelerated

Between LexisNexis, Thomson Reuters, and Clio, the major platforms absorbed six AI startups this quarter. Point solutions are becoming features. If you’re evaluating standalone AI tools, check whether your existing platform is about to ship the same capability.

4. Open-Source Legal AI Arrived

Open-source models matching commercial tools on benchmarks is a structural shift. It means the competitive advantage isn’t the AI model — it’s what you build around it. Data, workflows, and domain expertise matter more than raw model performance.

5. Regulation Got Specific

ABA ethics opinions, state bar CLE requirements, EU AI Act enforcement, UK SRA guidance — Q2 was the quarter where “someone should regulate this” turned into actual regulation. The compliance landscape for legal AI is real now.

What to Watch in Q3

The UPL Reckoning: The LawyerBot prosecution will have a chilling effect on consumer-facing legal AI apps. Expect more enforcement actions and clearer lines around what AI can and can’t do for non-lawyers.

AI-First Firms Emerge: Watch for the first law firms built from scratch around AI workflows — not legacy firms adding AI, but new entrants designed with AI at the core. At least two are launching in Q3.

Client Pressure Increases: In-house legal departments will start requiring AI-assisted workflows as part of outside counsel engagement terms. If your firm can’t demonstrate AI capability, you’ll lose panels.

Your Q3 Toolkit

Start the Quarter With the Right Tools

Everything you need is on LegalTech AI Hub, and it’s all free:

• Clause Checker — Instant clause analysis
• Doc Diff — Side-by-side document comparison

Visit LegalTech AI Hub →

Thank you for reading The Docket for 12 weeks. Q3 starts next Wednesday. We’re not slowing down — the industry certainly isn’t. See you then.
